originalTitle: Women secretly filmed, then ridiculed and abused online
title: Let's Be Kind Online: A Story from Kenya and Ghana
content:
Hello, Super Reporters!

Hello, my amazing friends! Welcome to our special news report for kids, all about being kind and safe in our big African family. Today, we have an important story from two wonderful countries in Africa: Kenya and Ghana. It's a story that helps us learn about respecting each other, especially when we use computers and phones.

What Happened to Some Women?

Imagine you are playing or walking, and someone comes up to talk to you. That's okay, right? But what if that person secretly records you with a camera, even without asking? That's what happened to some women in Kenya and Ghana. A man from a faraway country called Russia came to visit. He talked to some women. But then, he filmed them with his camera without telling them. This made the women very sad and uncomfortable because they did not know they were being filmed.

What is a Video?

Do you know what a video is? A video is like a moving picture that you can watch on a TV, a phone, or a computer. Videos can be lots of fun! We watch cartoons, learn new things, or see our families in videos. But videos should always be made with everyone's permission. Did you know? The first videos were made a very, very long time ago! Like magic, pictures started to move!

Asking for Permission is Important!

Now, here is a very important word: **permission**. Permission means asking someone if it's okay to do something. Like asking your mum if you can have a biscuit, or asking your friend if you can play with their toy. It is super important to always ask for permission before you film someone. And it's also important to ask before you share a picture or video of someone. This is called respecting their space and their feelings. If someone doesn't say "yes," then it's not okay to film them or share their picture. Their "no" means "no."

Why is it Wrong to Film Secretly?

Filming someone secretly is not kind. It's like someone reading your diary without you knowing! Everyone has a right to their own private space. We call this **privacy**. When someone films you without asking, they are not respecting your privacy. Imagine if someone filmed you making a funny face, and then everyone at school saw it without you wanting them to. You might feel embarrassed or sad. That's why asking is so, so important!

What Happened Next Online?

After the man secretly filmed the women, he put these videos on the internet. The internet is like a giant, magical library and playground where people all over the world can share and see things. When the videos were online, many people saw them. Some people started saying unkind things about the women. They made fun of them. This is called **online abuse** or **ridicule**. It means using the internet to say mean things that hurt people's feelings. Did you know? The internet connects almost everyone on Earth! It's like a big spiderweb of information!

Being Kind Everywhere, Even Online

It's important to be kind to everyone, whether you are playing with them in person or seeing them online. Just like we learn to use polite words like "please" and "thank you," we also need to be polite and kind when we are online. If you see something online that is unkind, or if someone is saying mean things, it's not okay to join in. It's much better to be a good friend and stand up for what is right, or tell a grown-up. Did you know? Every day, millions of kind messages are shared online! Let's add more kindness!

What Can We Do?

So, what can we learn from this story, my little reporters? 1. **Always Ask for Permission:** Before you take a picture or video of anyone, always ask them first. If they say no, that's okay! Just don't do it. 2. **Think Before You Share:** Before you put anything online – a picture, a video, or even words – think: "Is this kind? Is this respectful? Would I like it if someone did this to me?" 3. **Tell a Grown-up:** If you ever see something online that makes you feel scared, sad, or uncomfortable, tell a grown-up you trust right away. It could be your mum, dad, a teacher, or another family member. They are there to help you stay safe. 4. **Be a Digital Superhero:** You can be a digital superhero by always being kind online and by helping others be kind too! If you see someone being mean, you can show them how to be nice. Did you know? Your grown-ups can help you understand how to use the internet safely. They are your safety guides!

Our African Family Cares

In Africa, we believe in **Ubuntu**, which means "I am because we are." It means we are all connected, and we should care for each other. This includes caring for each other online too. Let's make sure that our digital world is a happy and safe place for everyone. Remember, your kindness can make a big difference. Let's be respectful, ask for permission, and be superheroes of kindness online and everywhere else! Stay safe, my friends!
summary: A story from Kenya and Ghana teaches us that it's important to always ask for permission before filming someone and to be kind and safe when using the internet, reporting anything mean to a trusted grown-up.
whyMatters: This story matters because everyone deserves to be treated with respect and kindness, both in person and online, and we must learn how to stay safe and make good choices on the internet.

originalTitle: South Africa's exclusion from G7 summit no surprise, says Ramaphosa
title: Our President and the Big Countries' Meeting!
content:
Hello, amazing friends! Are you ready for a story about our wonderful country, South Africa, and some other countries far, far away?

A Special Invitation

Guess what? Sometimes grown-ups who lead countries have big meetings. It's like when your teachers and parents meet to talk about important things for your school. Our wonderful President, Mr. Cyril Ramaphosa, was going to go to one of these very important meetings. It was going to be in a beautiful country called France. France is very far from South Africa, and it's known for yummy bread and a tall tower called the Eiffel Tower! The President of France, Mr. Macron, had invited our President Ramaphosa. This was a very special invitation!

What is the G7 Club?

Imagine you have a secret club with your best friends. These friends are very good at something, like playing games or drawing amazing pictures. The G7 is a bit like a club for some of the biggest and richest countries in the whole wide world! G7 means 'Group of Seven' because there are seven countries in this special club. They meet to talk about big problems that affect everyone, like how to help people, make sure everyone has food, and protect our amazing planet. They want to make the world a better place. Did you know? The G7 countries are Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, the United Kingdom, and the United States. That's a lot of important countries all working together!

Who is Our President?

Our President is Mr. Cyril Ramaphosa. He is the leader of our amazing country, South Africa. His job is to make sure everyone in South Africa is safe and happy. He works very, very hard every day to make our country strong and fair for all of us. He also talks to leaders from other countries to make friends and help South Africa grow bigger and better. Did you know? South Africa has many beautiful animals, like lions, elephants, and giraffes! Our country is special because it has so much wildlife.

Oh Dear! The Invitation Changed

So, our President was all set to go to this big G7 meeting in France. Everyone thought it was a great idea because South Africa is a very important country, not just in Africa, but in the whole world! But then, something a little bit tricky happened. It seems like the invitation changed! Our President was suddenly not going to the meeting anymore. It was a bit like when you plan to play with a friend, but then they suddenly can't come over. Everyone wondered why this happened.

A Story About Big Friends and Different Ideas

Some grown-ups who know a lot about countries said that another big country, the United States of America, had something to do with it. The leader of the United States at that time was President Trump. Sometimes, countries don't always agree on everything. It's a bit like when you and your friends have different ideas about what game to play, or which movie to watch. Sometimes, one friend might say, "If we play that game, I'm not playing!" Well, something like that happened with the countries. Reports said that President Trump told France that if President Ramaphosa came to the meeting, he might not come himself! Oh dear! That's a strong thing to say. It was like one big friend saying to another big friend, "If *they* come, *I* won't come!" So, the President of France, Mr. Macron, had to make a very tough choice. He didn't want the President of the United States to boycott (which means to not come) the meeting. So, in the end, President Ramaphosa's invitation was changed. Did you know? The United States of America is a very, very big country, and it's on a different continent from Africa! It has famous places like Disneyland and the Statue of Liberty.

Our President Was Not Surprised

When President Ramaphosa heard about this, he said he was not surprised. He is a very smart and calm leader. He understands that sometimes in the world of grown-up politics, things can be a bit complicated. He knows that powerful countries sometimes make decisions that affect others. He said that South Africa is still strong and important, even if we don't go to every single meeting. He always thinks about what is best for South Africa and all its people. Did you know? Our President Ramaphosa loves to help children learn and grow. He often visits schools and talks to kids, just like you!

Why South Africa is Super Important

South Africa is a very, very important country, not just for us, but for all of Africa and the whole world! We have many wonderful people, rich cultures, and beautiful lands. We also have many smart people who can help solve big problems, like how to keep our planet clean and how to make sure everyone has a good job. When South Africa speaks, other countries listen because we are a voice for many people. Did you know? Africa is a giant continent with 54 different countries! South Africa is at the very bottom tip of Africa, like a proud foot for the whole continent.

Working Together is Key!

Even if our President didn't go to this one meeting, it doesn't mean we stop being friends with other countries. We still want to work together to make the world a better place. We want to help people, make sure everyone has enough food to eat, and protect our amazing animals and nature. That's what grown-up leaders do – they try to find ways to help everyone, even when things get a little tricky or they don't agree perfectly. Did you know? South Africa is famous for its beautiful flowers, especially a special one called the Protea! It's our national flower.

Let's Keep Learning and Being Friends!

So, that's the story! Our President is a strong leader, and South Africa is a strong country. We will keep making friends and working hard with other countries. And you, our wonderful children, are the future leaders of South Africa! Keep learning about the world, be kind to your friends, and always ask questions. You are all amazing!
summary: Our South African President was going to a big meeting with other country leaders, but he didn't go because another big leader said he might not come if our President was there.
whyMatters: It's important because it shows that even if leaders don't agree sometimes, South Africa is still strong and important, and we keep working to make friends with everyone and help the world.

originalTitle: Tiger Woods charged with driving under influence after crash
title: A Famous Golfer's Car Story and Why Safety Rules Matter!
content:
Hello from Your Africa Reporter!

Hi everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you a story about a very famous sports star. His name is Tiger Woods. He lives very, very far away in a place called Florida. Florida is a state in a big country called America, and it's full of sunshine and beaches! You might know Tiger Woods because he is super, super good at a game called golf.

What Happened to Tiger Woods?

One day, something happened to Tiger Woods. He was driving his car. Cars are wonderful for taking us places, but they need grown-ups to drive them very carefully. On this day, his car had a small accident. His car bumped into something, and it rolled a little bit. But here's the good news: Tiger Woods was okay! He did not get badly hurt, and no one else was hurt either. This is the most important thing when a car has an accident – that everyone is safe. The police came to help him after the crash.

Did You Know?

Did you know? Golf is a game where people use special sticks, called clubs, to hit a small white ball into a little hole very far away! It takes a lot of practice and skill to be as good as Tiger Woods.

Understanding "Driving Under the Influence"

Now, here's a part of the story that teaches us about grown-up rules and safety. When grown-ups drive cars, they have a very important job. They need to be super careful and pay attention to everything around them. They watch for other cars, people walking, traffic lights, and road signs. There are special rules to help grown-ups drive safely. One very important rule is that grown-ups should never drive if they have taken something that makes their head feel fuzzy, sleepy, or not very clear. Sometimes, certain drinks or even some medicines can make grown-ups feel this way. The police found that Tiger Woods had something in his body that made it hard for him to drive his car perfectly safely. When this happens, it's called "driving under the influence." It just means he wasn't able to drive his car as perfectly as he should have been because of something he had taken. Because of this, the police said he was charged with "driving under the influence."

Why are Driving Rules So Important?

Why do we have these rules? Because everyone needs to be safe on the roads! Imagine if drivers were not paying attention, or if their heads felt fuzzy. It could be very, very dangerous for them and for everyone else. Roads are busy places, and we all need to share them safely. Police officers are very important people who help us. They make sure that everyone follows the rules, especially rules about driving, so that our towns and cities stay safe for everyone. They were there to make sure that everything was okay after Tiger Woods' car accident and to understand what happened.

Did You Know?

Did you know? In many countries, like ours, you must always wear a seatbelt when you are in a car, whether you are a grown-up or a child! Seatbelts are like a gentle hug that keeps you safe in your seat if the car stops suddenly or has an accident. Did you know? Police officers are sometimes called "peace officers" because they help keep peace and order in our communities.

What Can We Learn from This Story?

This story about Tiger Woods, the famous golfer, teaches us some important lessons. First, even grown-ups, and even famous heroes, can sometimes make mistakes. Nobody is perfect, and sometimes people don't make the best choices. But the most important thing is to learn from our mistakes. When we learn from a mistake, it helps us make better choices next time. For grown-ups who drive, it means always making sure they are clear-headed and focused when they are behind the steering wheel. For us, as children, it's a good reminder to always follow safety rules. That means wearing your seatbelt, holding a grown-up's hand when you cross the road, and never playing where cars drive. Everyone, big or small, has a role in staying safe and helping others stay safe.

Did You Know?

Did you know? Florida, where this happened, is very far from Africa! It's across a huge ocean, and it would take many, many hours to fly there in an airplane! Did you know? Cars have many safety features, like special cushions called airbags that pop out to protect people in a crash. These, along with seatbelts, help keep people safe. So, remember, rules are there to keep us safe and sound. Let's all try our best to make good choices every day, just like we hope everyone driving a car will too!
summary: Famous golfer Tiger Woods was involved in a car accident in Florida and was charged because he wasn't able to drive safely, teaching us all about important rules for grown-ups when driving and the importance of safety for everyone.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us that even famous grown-ups can make mistakes, and it reminds everyone how important it is for drivers to be super careful and follow rules to keep themselves and everyone else safe on the roads.

originalTitle: Who was England's attacking spark? How England's players rated
title: Football Fun! Who Was England's Brightest Star?
content:
Hello, amazing friends in Africa! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, ready to share some exciting football news. We love football here, don't we? It's a game full of running, kicking, and cheering! Today, we're talking about a special football match. It was a game between the country England and the country Uruguay. It was a super fun game where players showed their best moves! A special reporter watched everyone very carefully. He looked at who played really, really well.

What is a Friendly Match?

Imagine you want to be super good at kicking a ball. You don't play a big game right away, right? You practice! A friendly match is just like that. It's like a practice game for big football teams. It's just for fun and to get better, not to win a big cup. Teams play friendly matches to try new ways of playing. They learn how to play together even better. It helps them get ready for bigger, more important games in the future. It’s all about teamwork and learning. Did you know? The very first international football match ever played was in 1872! It was between Scotland and England. That was a very, very long time ago!

Meeting the England Team!

In football, every player has a special job. It's like in a big family, everyone helps in a different way! Some players try to score goals, some try to stop the other team from scoring, and some help in the middle. The England team has many amazing players. They wear white jerseys when they play. When they played Uruguay, everyone watched to see who would shine the brightest. Who would be the 'attacking spark'? We will find out! Did you know? Football is the most popular sport in the world! Millions of people play and watch it every day.

Who Played Well for England?

Let's talk about some of the players and how well they played! It’s like giving stars for good work. Some players really made the game exciting. First, there was **Speedy Musa**! Musa plays as an attacker for England. He is super, super fast, like a cheetah! He runs with the ball so quickly. He makes the other team's players chase him. Musa tried very hard to kick the ball into the goal. He was a real 'attacking spark' in the game! He ran past many players and tried to score for England. He looked very happy to play football. We give Speedy Musa 5 shining stars for his amazing speed and effort! Then there was **Clever Ama**! Ama is a midfielder. That means she plays in the middle of the field. She passes the ball to her friends. She also helps get the ball back if the other team has it. Ama is very smart with the ball. She knows exactly where to send it! She helped the team move the ball forward from the back to the front. Ama made many good passes. She helped England keep the ball a lot. We give Clever Ama 4 happy faces for her clever passing and teamwork! And let's not forget **Strong Kofi**! Kofi is a defender. His job is super important. He stops the other team, Uruguay, from scoring goals. He is brave and strong. He blocks the ball and makes sure it stays far away from England's goal. Kofi was like a big, strong wall! He stopped many of Uruguay's players. Kofi made sure the goalkeeper had less work to do. He protected the goal very well. We give Strong Kofi 4 big thumbs up for his strong defense! At the very back, we had **Safe Zola**! Zola is the goalkeeper. His job is to catch or block any ball that tries to go into England's net. He jumps high and dives low. Zola saved many shots from Uruguay. He kept England safe from goals. Zola was like a superhero guarding the net! He caught balls that looked impossible to catch. We give Safe Zola 5 protective shields for his amazing saves!

What is an 'Attacking Spark'?

An 'attacking spark' is a very fun way to describe a player! Think about a firework! When it lights up, everyone goes 'Wow!' An 'attacking spark' in football is a player who makes exciting things happen when their team tries to score a goal. They run fast, they dribble smartly around other players, and they shoot powerfully towards the goal. They are the ones who bring energy and danger to the other team's goal. They make the game super fun to watch! Speedy Musa was a great 'attacking spark' in this game. Did you know? The fastest football shot ever recorded was over 210 kilometers per hour! That's super, super fast!

How Do We Rate Players?

So, how do special reporters know who played well? It's like how your teacher gives you stars or stickers for good work! The reporter watches the whole game very, very carefully. They look at many things. They watch how many good passes a player makes. They see how many times a player tackles the ball fairly. They count how many shots a player takes at the goal. They also see if a player is helping their friends on the team. It helps coaches know who is doing great and who might need more practice. It’s like grading homework, but for football! This helps teams get even better for the next match. Did you know? Football coaches spend many hours watching old games to help their team learn and improve! So, my friends, that was our look at England's friendly match against Uruguay. It was full of exciting moments and amazing players like Speedy Musa, Clever Ama, Strong Kofi, and Safe Zola. Keep kicking that ball and cheering for your favorite teams! Goodbye for now, from your reporter in Africa!
